lks2.objective: Strike the ball off the tee through one of the gates — develop placement and shot direction.
successCriteria: Strikes the ball cleanly off the tee
Holds the bat with two hands

uks2.objective: Read the fielder's position and strike into open space — vary direction and power to find the unguarded gate.
successCriteria: Looks at fielder before each strike
Varies which gate is targeted across the 5 strikes
Adjusts power to match the gate distance

equipment.item: Plastic / soft cricket bat (or tennis racket / rounders bat)

space.description: Rectangular pitch with 3 cone gates spread along the far end. Batter at one end on the tee, fielder defending the gates.

steps: Pair children up — one batter, one fielder.
The batter hits the ball off a tee/cone trying to place it through one of the gates.
The fielder has to try and stop the ball going through the gate.
The batter has 5 strikes then switch roles.
questions: What will help you choose which way to strike the ball?
What advice would you give your partner to help them improve?
What could you do to improve your score?

step.task: Fielder can bowl the ball to batter (live, not off the tee)
step.equipment: Batter can choose equipment (tennis racket, cricket bat, rounders bat)
step.people: Increase gates with more or fewer fielders

errors.fix: Cue 'front foot DOWN the line, not across'
Cue 'eyes on the ball until you HEAR it leave'

media.title: Through the Gate — Just Play Sports
